description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular agricultural building with a prominent, steeply pitched hip roof clad in reddish-brown shingles. The roof overhangs the structure, providing shade and protection to the wooden plank walls below. The walls are constructed from horizontal timber boards, giving the building a rustic and sturdy appearance. The front side features an open entrance supported by wooden posts, allowing easy access for livestock and ventilation. There are no visible windows or decorative elements, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The overall structure is robust, designed to shelter cattle and withstand outdoor conditions.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a steep red hip roof and open front entrance, built for durability and livestock shelter.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open entrance supported by wooden posts beneath the large red hip roof.
description_view_1: This side view displays the uninterrupted wooden plank wall beneath the overhanging roof, with no visible openings.
description_view_2: Another side view, nearly identical to view 1, showing the continuous wooden wall and roof overhang.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals a solid wooden wall with no doors or windows, topped by the same red hip roof.
attribute_door_style: open front entrance, no visible doors
